title: Suribran Tu

{{Quote|Suribran Tu is a butcher. His people ''hate'' him. Keep him in power and you ''prove'' there is no difference between the Confederacy and the Republic. And if he's willing to sell-out the Republic, he'll sell out ''you''.|Quinlan Vos, to Count Dooku|<ref name="J:CD" />}}
'''Suribran Tu''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Ishi Tib/Legends|Ishi Tib]] [[Politics/Legends|politician]] who lived during the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] just before the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Tu was ruling his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Tibrin/Legends|Tibrin]] as a dictator with the backing of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]—an [[The galaxy/Legends|interstellar]] superpower that operated under the ideals of democracy and freedom. Tu was left to conduct his governance as he saw fit, and his [[Suribran Tu's regime|regime]] ruled with an iron fist and ultimately [[Death/Legends|killed]] a considerable number of its own people through political [[Assassin/Legends|assassinations]].

With the outbreak of the Clone Wars in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the citizens of Tibrin [[Tibrin revolution|placed their loyalty]] with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], causing the separatist government's [[Head of State/Legends|Head of State]], [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], to travel to the [[Planet/Legends|world]] and meet with Tu. The Ishi Tib [[premier]] offered to switch allegiance from the Republic to the Confederacy and rule Tibrin at Dooku's command, but the Confederate leader instead executed Tu when [[Dark Acolyte/Legends|Dark Acolyte]] [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] suggested that murdering Tu would engender the loyalty of the people of Tibrin.

==Biography==
{{Quote|So. ''You're'' now in charge, Count Dooku, as ''I'' once was. All right. I'm ''practical''. I can accept ''that''. ''You're'' practical, too, I think. You ''need'' me. I am useful. I know how to ''rule'' here. So now I'll rule for ''you''. Republic, Confederacy—no difference to me. I think, as practical beings, we can come to some agreement, Count. What do you think?|Suribran Tu, to Count Dooku|<ref name="J:CD" />}}
Suribran Tu was an [[Ishi Tib/Legends|Ishi Tib]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] native to his [[Species/Legends|species]]' [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Tibrin/Legends|Tibrin]].<ref name="CSWE" /> In the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],<ref name="NEC" /> Tu lived as a [[Politics/Legends|politician]] and eventually came to be the [[premier]]<ref name="FF 134">{{FactFile|134|TYR 1-2|Rise of a Tyrant}}</ref> of his home [[Planet/Legends|planet]] and a dictator to his people.<ref name="NEC" /> Although his [[Suribran Tu's regime|regime]] was marked by low approval from the Ishi Tib and an iron-fist approach to governance, it held the support of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="J:CD" />—the preeminent [[The galaxy/Legends|interstellar]] government that dedicated its rule to the ideals of freedom and democracy.<ref name="Republic">{{DB|organization|galacticrepublic|Galactic Republic}}</ref> Between [[24 BBY/Legends|24]] and [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], a separatist government known as the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] formed in opposition to the Republic, which was increasingly seen as corrupt and stagnant.<ref name="NEC" /> By the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the two superpowers in 22 BBY, the Ishi Tib populace of Tibrin was calling for their planet to join the Confederate uprising under the leadership of [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]].<ref name="CSWE" /><ref name="J:CD" />

[[File:Cw_vol4_04.jpg|thumb|left|290px|Dooku beheads Suribran Tu.]]
Tu resisted the will of his people and instead employed strong-arm tactics and ordered many political [[Assassin/Legends|assassinations]] to maintain his own position, as well as his planet's allegiance to the Republic.<ref name="CSWE" /> Dooku eventually learned of the situation on Tibrin and so traveled to the planet<ref name="J:CD" /> on 21.38 BBY—seven and a half [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]]—<ref name="NEC" />with a posse of his [[Dark Acolyte/Legends|Dark Acolytes]] to negotiate with Tu. The Confederate [[Head of State/Legends|Head of State's]] arrival on the world was met with [[Tibrin revolution|jubilation from the populace]], who organized a welcoming rally outside [[Suribran Tu's palace|Tu's palace]] while the Count and their dictator met within.<ref name="J:CD" /> During the meeting, Dooku requested the rights to use Tibrin as a Separatist base,<ref name="CSWE" /> but Tu could see where the balance of power lay in the negotiations—given the Confederacy's support from his people and the presence of the Count's Acolytes, Tu understood that Tibrin now belonged to Dooku. The Ishi Tib explained to the Count that he could accept this new reality, and that it would be in the best interest of both parties if the Confederacy left him in charge of Tibrin so that he might be able to rule his people in Dooku's name.<ref name="J:CD" />

Dooku sought advice on the matter from two of his Dark Acolytes—former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Kadrian Sey]] and [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]]. While Sey argued that leaving Tu in charge of Tibrin would allow for continuity of administration and experienced leadership, Vos asserted that leaving the dictator in place would prove to the populace that there was no difference between the Confederacy and the Republic. Vos further insinuated that Tu's willingness to swap allegiances could be a liability, an argument to which the Count was receptive. Dooku agreed with Vos and so abruptly [[Death/Legends|executed]] Suribran Tu by [[Sai cha/Legends|beheading]] the dictator with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. The Count appointed his student [[Sora Bulq/Legends|Sora Bulq]] as Tibrin's provisional [[Governor/Legends|governor]], with the duty of killing all members of Tu's inner circle, including political and military [[Advisor/Legends|advisors]], as well as family members. Acolytes Sey and [[Tol Skorr]] took Tu's body to be displayed to the public fashion; Dooku ordered that the dictator's head be mounted somewhere highly visible. Meanwhile, the Count greeted the crowds outside the palace, who welcomed him as a hero<ref name="J:CD" /> and eagerly joined the Confederate cause.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|DOOKU! DOOKU! LIBERATOR!|Cries of cheer from the Ishi Tib populace following Tu's execution|<ref name="J:CD" />}}
[[File:Tibrin_Seps.jpg|thumb|right|290px|The people of Tibrin cheer Dooku's execution of Tu.]]
Although Suribran Tu was seen as a cruel dictator by both his people<ref name="NEC" /> and later historians, he merely believed himself to be a practical being.<ref name="J:CD" /> Because the Republic government had propped up Tu's regime despite the dictator's transgressions against his own people, Tu chose to remain loyal to the ancient democracy and fight his own people and their Separatist sympathies.<ref name="CSWE" /> These actions earned him the scorn of the Ishi Tib and the suspicion of Count Dooku and Quinlan Vos, who saw his reign as a prime example of why the Republic had to be replaced. When the dictator finally chose to defect to the Confederacy, he did so on the grounds that both he and Dooku were reasonable, and that the two could both be satisfied with Tu serving as a proxy governor. Dooku disagreed and was hailed as a liberator for his execution of Suribran Tu.<ref name="J:CD" />

==Behind the scenes==
Suribran Tu first appeared in ''[[Jedi: Count Dooku]]'', a [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] written by [[John Ostrander]] and illustrated by [[Jan Duursema]].<ref name="J:CD" /> The comic was published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] and published on [[December 3]], [[2003]].<ref name="Release date">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/12-377/Star-Wars-Jedi----Dooku|text=STAR WARS: JEDI -- DOOKU}}</ref>
